official_name: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers
notes: IEEE produces ethical AI and technology governance standards including the 7000 series, Ethically Aligned Design framework, and standards addressing AI trustworthiness, bias, and privacy. IEEE standards are paywalled — access requires purchase from IEEE Xplore or the IEEE Standards Association.

data.official_name: IEEE 754 - Standard for Floating-Point Arithmetic
data.notes: Paywalled. Defines binary and decimal floating-point arithmetic — number representation, rounding, exceptions. Foundational to numerical reproducibility in ML/AI systems.

data.official_name: IEEE 1471 - Recommended Practice for Architectural Description of Software-Intensive Systems
data.notes: Paywalled. Superseded by ISO/IEC/IEEE 42010. Specifies requirements for describing system/software architectures; still referenced by older architecture documentation practices.

match_nodes.description: IEEE 1609 - Wireless Access in Vehicular Environments (WAVE)

data.official_name: IEEE 1609 family - Wireless Access in Vehicular Environments (WAVE)
data.notes: Paywalled. Family of standards for V2X communications: 1609.0 (architecture), 1609.2 (security), 1609.3 (networking), 1609.4 (multi-channel). Relevant to AI in connected/autonomous vehicles.

data.official_name: IEEE 2840 - Standard for Responsible AI Licensing
data.notes: Paywalled. Focuses on responsible AI licensing terms. Note: the README in the CSA dataset describes 2840 as 'AI Risk Management' — verify against the published title at IEEE Xplore before relying on it.

data.official_name: IEEE 2857 - Privacy Engineering for AI Systems
data.notes: Paywalled. Framework for incorporating privacy considerations throughout the AI system lifecycle (design through operation). Complements ISO/IEC 27701 and 29100.

data.official_name: IEEE 2858 - Privacy Engineering Objectives
data.notes: Paywalled. Defines privacy objectives for systems processing personally identifiable information. Companion to 2857 (engineering framework) and 2859 (process).

data.official_name: IEEE 2859 - Privacy Engineering Process
data.notes: Paywalled. Defines a process for privacy engineering in systems that process PII. Companion to 2857 (framework) and 2858 (objectives).

data.official_name: IEEE 5339 - Standard for AI Management System Requirements
data.notes: Paywalled. Requirements for AI management systems within organizations — complementary to ISO/IEC 42001 and IEEE 42001 (the latter being an adoption of the former).

data.official_name: IEEE 7000 - Ethical System Design

versions_available.note: IEEE 7000-2021 standard for ethical system design.

data.official_name: IEEE 7001 - Transparency of Autonomous Systems

versions_available.note: IEEE 7001-2021 standard for transparency of autonomous systems.

data.official_name: IEEE 7002 - Data Privacy Process

versions_available.note: IEEE 7002-2022 standard for data privacy process.

data.official_name: IEEE 7010 - Well-being Metrics for AI

versions_available.note: IEEE 7010-2020 standard for well-being metrics for AI.

match_nodes.description: IEEE 7011 - Process of Identifying and Rating the Trustworthiness of News Sources

data.official_name: IEEE 7011 - Standard for the Process of Identifying and Rating the Trustworthiness of News Sources
data.notes: Paywalled. Defines processes for assessing and rating the trustworthiness of news sources. Addresses misinformation and media integrity challenges.

versions_available.note: IEEE 7011-2024 standard for identifying and rating trustworthiness of news sources.

match_nodes.description: IEEE 7012 - Machine Readable Personal Privacy Terms

data.official_name: IEEE 7012 - Standard for Machine Readable Personal Privacy Terms
data.notes: Paywalled. Defines a machine-readable format for personal privacy terms, enabling automated privacy negotiation between individuals and organizations.

versions_available.note: IEEE 7012-2024 standard for machine readable personal privacy terms.

data.official_name: ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207 - Systems and software engineering — Software life cycle processes
data.notes: Paywalled. Joint IEEE/ISO/IEC standard defining software lifecycle processes — acquisition, supply, development, operation, maintenance, disposal. Foundational for software engineering process discipline.

data.official_name: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 - Systems and software engineering — System life cycle processes
data.notes: Paywalled. Joint IEEE/ISO/IEC standard defining system lifecycle processes — companion to 12207 but at the systems (not just software) level. Applies to AI systems and complex cloud infrastructure engineering.

match_nodes.description: IEEE 24028 - Ethical Considerations in AI and Autonomous Systems (Transparency)

data.official_name: IEEE 24028 - Standard for Ethical Considerations in AI and Autonomous Systems (Transparency)
data.notes: Paywalled. IEEE adoption of ISO/IEC TR 24028:2020 on trustworthiness in AI. Addresses transparency, explainability, and accountability in AI systems. Note: distinct from but aligned with ISO/IEC TR 24028 — IEEE publishes its own edition.

versions_available.note: IEEE adoption of ISO/IEC TR 24028:2020 on AI trustworthiness.

match_nodes.description: IEEE 24029-1 - Assessment of Biases in AI Systems (Part 1)

data.official_name: IEEE 24029-1 - Assessment of Biases in AI Systems and Methods for Mitigation (Part 1)
data.notes: Paywalled. IEEE adoption of ISO/IEC TR 24029-1:2021 on robustness assessment of neural networks. Part 1 covers assessment methods. Note: distinct from but aligned with ISO/IEC 24029 — IEEE publishes its own edition.

versions_available.note: IEEE adoption of ISO/IEC 24029-1:2021 on neural network robustness assessment.

data.official_name: IEEE 24029-2 - Assessment of Robustness of Neural Networks Part 2: Methodology for the use of formal methods
data.notes: Paywalled. IEEE adoption of ISO/IEC 24029-2. Part 2 covers formal-methods-based robustness assessment of neural networks (Part 1 covers statistical methods). Note: companion to 24029-1.

data.official_name: IEEE 24030 - Use cases and applications for AI systems
data.notes: Paywalled. IEEE adoption of ISO/IEC TR 24030:2021. Catalogue of AI use cases across domains (healthcare, finance, manufacturing, etc.) plus a framework for documenting new use cases.

data.official_name: IEEE 42001 - AI Management System
data.notes: Paywalled. IEEE adoption of ISO/IEC 42001:2023. Requirements for establishing, implementing, maintaining and continually improving an AI management system (AIMS). Note: this is the IEEE edition of the same standard also published by ISO (see secid:control/iso.org/42001). Same technical content, different publication channel.

versions_available.note: IEEE adoption of ISO/IEC 42001:2023. First international AI management system standard.

data.official_name: IEEE 802.11 - Wireless LAN Medium Access Control (MAC) and Physical Layer (PHY) Specifications
data.notes: Paywalled. Family of standards for wireless local area networks (Wi-Fi). Security amendments (WPA2, WPA3) are critical for protecting cloud/AI system access.

data.official_name: IEEE 802.1X - Port-Based Network Access Control
data.notes: Paywalled. Authentication framework for network access (PNAC). Widely used for enterprise Wi-Fi (EAP), wired LAN access, and identity-based network segmentation.

versions_available.note: First edition of Ethically Aligned Design.

match_nodes.description: IEEE TR 24027 - Bias in AI Systems (Technical Report)

data.official_name: IEEE TR 24027 - Bias in AI Systems and AI-Aided Decision Making
data.notes: Paywalled technical report (not a standard). Comprehensive treatment of bias types in AI and mitigation strategies. IEEE adoption of ISO/IEC TR 24027:2021.
